epigraph.text: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord.

items: By sides: scalene, isosceles, equilateral
By angles: acute, right, obtuse
Triangle angle sum: $180°$
Congruent figures have the same size and shape
Corresponding parts match based on vertex order
Congruence is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive
SSS: three pairs of congruent sides
SAS: two sides and included angle
ASA: two angles and included side
AAS: two angles and non-included side
NOT valid: SSA, AAA
Corresponding Parts of Congruent Triangles are Congruent
Used after proving triangles congruent
For right triangles only
Hypotenuse and leg congruent $\Rightarrow$ triangles congruent

content.text: Classify each triangle by sides and angles.

items: Sides: 3, 4, 5; one angle is $90°$
Sides: 7, 7, 10; one angle is $100°$

content.text:
If $\triangle RST \cong \triangle XYZ$, complete each 
statement.

items: $\angle S \cong \underline{\quad}$
$\overline{RT} \cong \underline{\quad}$
$\triangle TSR \cong \underline{\quad}$

content.text:
State which postulate or theorem proves the triangles 
congruent, or state "cannot be determined."

items:
Given: $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{DE}$, $\overline{BC} 
 \cong \overline{EF}$, $\overline{CA} \cong \overline{FD}$

Given: $\angle A \cong \angle D$, $\angle B \cong \angle E$, 
 $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{DE}$

Given: $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{DE}$, $\overline{BC} 
 \cong \overline{EF}$, $\angle C \cong \angle F$

Given: Right angles at $C$ and $F$, $\overline{AB} \cong 
 \overline{DE}$, $\overline{AC} \cong \overline{DF}$

content.text:
Prove: $\triangle ABE \cong \triangle CDE$ (where $E$ is the 
intersection of $\overline{AC}$ and $\overline{BD}$)
Given: $M$ is the midpoint of $\overline{PQ}$ and $\overline{RS}$

items:
Classify a triangle with sides 8, 8, 6 and angles $70°$, 
 $70°$, $40°$.

If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle PQR$, name all pairs of 
 corresponding parts.

What additional information is needed to prove $\triangle JKL 
 \cong \triangle MNO$ by SAS if $\overline{JK} \cong \overline{MN}$ 
 and $\angle K \cong \angle N$?

State which congruence criterion applies: Given $\angle A 
 \cong \angle D$, $\angle C \cong \angle F$, $\overline{BC} \cong 
 \overline{EF}$.

Write a proof.
 
 Given: $\overline{WX} \cong \overline{YZ}$, $\overline{WZ} \cong 
 \overline{YX}$
 
 Prove: $\angle W \cong \angle Y$

In right triangles $ABC$ and $DEF$ with right angles at $C$ 
 and $F$, $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{DE}$ and $\overline{BC} 
 \cong \overline{EF}$. Can you prove the triangles congruent? If 
 so, how?

True or False: If two triangles have all corresponding angles 
 congruent, the triangles must be congruent.

Write a proof using CPCTC.
 
 Given: $\overline{AB} \cong \overline{CB}$, $D$ is the midpoint 
 of $\overline{AC}$
 
 Prove: $\overline{BD} \perp \overline{AC}$

content.text:
Congruence is one of the most powerful concepts in geometry. With 
just a few pieces of information—three sides, or two sides and an 
angle, or two angles and a side—we can conclude that entire triangles 
are identical in every respect.

This economy of conditions reflects deep structural truths. A triangle 
is a *rigid* figure: once you fix three sides, the angles are 
determined. This rigidity is why triangles appear in bridges, roof 
trusses, and other structures that must not flex.

The CPCTC principle extends this power: once we know triangles are 
congruent, we gain knowledge about all six of their parts simultaneously. 
A single proof unlocks multiple conclusions.

In the chapters ahead, you will study similarity (same shape, different 
size) and explore special triangles and quadrilaterals. The reasoning 
patterns you've developed here—careful attention to what's given, 
strategic use of postulates and theorems, building conclusions 
step-by-step—will serve you throughout geometry and beyond.

Congruent triangles are identical in their 
essential properties. Consider what it means for created things to 
share essential properties with their Creator—or with each other. 
How does understanding geometric congruence illuminate theological 
concepts like the *imago Dei*?

marginNotes.text:
For the Lord gives wisdom; from his 
mouth come knowledge and understanding.

Great are the works of the Lord, studied 
by all who delight in them.

marginNotes.author: David Hilbert
marginNotes.text:
Mathematics knows no races or geographic 
boundaries.
